question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish() bean in MonitorConfiguration] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that triggers the .retry() operator on the Flux.generate() stream (which retries on any error from the generator), causing the generator to restart from scratch and reset all scenario state including the remaining counter and stopTime, effectively resetting the publish scenario's progress and potentially causing duplicate transaction submissions when the partition resolves?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: init() in MonitorProperties] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition during application startup that prevents the @PostConstruct init() method from completing (e.g., if it depends on network-resolved shard/realm values), causing the Spring context to fail to initialize and the entire MonitorApplication to crash, resulting in a complete monitoring blackout during the partition?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: refresh() in NodeSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronized keyword on refresh() combined with a network partition that causes the Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1L)) to hold the monitor lock indefinitely, blocking any other thread that calls refresh() (including the periodic validation Flux.interval()) from acquiring the lock, causing a deadlock-like starvation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getAddressBook() in NodeSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition to the mirror node REST API so that restApiClient.getNodes() returns an empty Flux (no nodes with non-empty serviceEndpoints), causing getAddressBook() to complete with zero nodes, and the switchIfEmpty fallback to monitorProperties.getNetwork().getNodes() to be used, potentially routing all traffic to hardcoded network nodes that may also be unreachable during the partition?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toNodeProperties() in NodeSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that results in the mirror node REST API returning NetworkNode objects with serviceEndpoints containing only TLS endpoints (port 50212) when the TlsMode is configured as PLAINTEXT, causing the tlsPredicate.test(s.getPort()) filter to reject all endpoints and toNodeProperties() to return an empty list, silently dropping all nodes from the address book?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validateNode() in NodeSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user cause a partial network partition where only the receipt query (getReceipt(client)) is blocked while the transaction submission (execute(client)) succeeds, causing the validateNode() method to hang at the receipt query until requestTimeout (default 15s) expires, and with maxThreads=25 parallel validators all hanging simultaneously, exhausting the validator thread pool?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: close() in TransactionPublisher] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that triggers application shutdown (e.g., health check failures causing Kubernetes to restart the pod), where the close() method attempts to close all cached clients but the clients Flux has never been subscribed to (due to the partition preventing initialization), causing the close() to silently do nothing and leaving gRPC channels open?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onErrorDropped hook in MonitorConfiguration] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that generates a flood of non-CompletionException errors dropped by the Hooks.onErrorDropped handler, causing the log.warn('onErrorDropped: {}') to be called at extremely high frequency, filling log storage and causing log4j/logback to block application threads waiting for log I/O, amplifying the denial-of-service impact of the partition?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribe() bean in MonitorConfiguration] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that terminates all gRPC subscriptions, triggering the doFinally(s -> log.warn('Stopped subscribe after {} signal', s)) handler, and since the subscribe() bean has destroyMethod='dispose' but the Disposable is already disposed by the error, the Spring context shutdown attempts to dispose an already-disposed Disposable, potentially causing a NullPointerException during graceful shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: health() in ReleaseHealthIndicator] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition between the monitor pod and the Kubernetes API server, so that getHelmRelease() throws an exception when calling client.pods().withName(hostname).get().getMetadata().getLabels(), causing the onErrorComplete() to swallow the error and switchIfEmpty(UNKNOWN) to return Health.unknown(), masking the real partition from health monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth() in ReleaseHealthIndicator] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition to the Kubernetes API that occurs after the initial cacheInvalidateIf(v -> false) caches a stale UNKNOWN health result, so that the cache.() with properties.getCacheExpiry() serves the stale UNKNOWN status indefinitely even after the partition resolves, preventing the health indicator from ever returning to UP without an application restart?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: query() in PrometheusApiClient] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition to the Prometheus endpoint so that prometheusClient.get().uri(...).retrieve().body() blocks for the full SimpleClientHttpRequestFactory readTimeout duration on every health check invocation, and since ImporterLagHealthIndicator.health() is synchronous (implements HealthIndicator not ReactiveHealthIndicator), the blocking call ties up a Spring web thread for the full timeout duration on every health check request?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: evaluate() in ImporterLagHealthIndicator] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that results in the Prometheus query returning a PrometheusQueryResponse with status='success' but an empty result list, causing getSeries() to return an empty list, evaluate() to return up() unconditionally, and the health indicator to report UP even though the importer is completely unreachable due to the partition?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isOtherClearlyBetter() in ImporterLagHealthIndicator] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that affects only the local cluster's importer, so that localLag exceeds thresholdSeconds but bestOtherLag is empty (because all other clusters are also unreachable via Prometheus), causing isOtherClearlyBetter() to return false and evaluate() to return up() even though the local cluster is clearly degraded, hiding the partition impact?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve() in RestApiClient] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the mirror node REST API return slow responses (just under the timeout threshold), causing the WebClient connection pool to fill up with in-flight requests, and since retrieve() uses .onErrorResume(Mono::error) which re-emits errors without transformation, a connection pool exhaustion error propagates up through the entire subscribe pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onPublish() in RestSubscriber] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that slows down the REST API, causing the RestSubscription sink's tryEmitNext() to fail (FAIL_NON_SERIALIZED or FAIL_OVERFLOW) because the sink buffer is full of unprocessed PublishResponse objects, silently dropping publish events without any error being recorded, causing the REST subscriber to appear healthy while actually missing all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: clientSubscribe() in RestSubscriber] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the REST API return HTTP 503 (not 404), so shouldRetry() returns false and the error is passed to subscription.onError() then swallowed by onErrorResume(e -> Mono.empty()), causing the subscription to silently skip all transactions during the partition without incrementing error counters or triggering health alerts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toResponse() in RestSubscriber] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that results in the REST API returning a TransactionByIdResponse with an empty transactions list, causing Iterables.getFirst(response.getTransactions(), null) to return null, setting consensusTimestamp to null in the SubscribeResponse, and causing SubscribeMetrics.onNext() to skip latency recording, making the partition's latency impact invisible in metrics?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: init() in NodeSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition during the very first Flux.interval(Duration.ZERO, ...) tick (which fires immediately at startup), so that the initial node validation fails and nodes remains empty, and the repeatWhen retry fires with retryBackoff delay (default 2 minutes), meaning the monitor is completely non-functional for up to 2 minutes after startup even if the partition resolves within seconds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: refresh() in NodeSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1L)).maxBackoff(Duration.ofSeconds(8L)) retry loop in refresh() consume a dedicated Schedulers.newSingle('nodes') thread indefinitely, and since a new single-thread scheduler is created on every refresh() call (not reused), repeated partition-induced refresh() calls create an unbounded number of idle scheduler threads?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish() in TransactionPublisher] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes execute(client, transaction) hang until the SDK's internal timeout, and since publishProperties.isAsync()=true uses Mono.fromFuture(executable.executeAsync(client)), the CompletableFuture never completes, causing the .timeout(properties.getTimeout()) operator to fire and emit a TimeoutException that is wrapped in PublishException, but the underlying SDK thread continues running, causing thread leaks?
